event: The Transfer of the Sacred Carpet (Mahmal) from Mecca to Cairo
symbols: mahmal (ornate camel litter) — the ceremonial conveyance symbolizing royal patronage of the Hajj pilgrimage
camel — the traditional beast of burden in Near Eastern culture, evoking biblical patriarchal journeys
procession — communal religious devotion and the public display of piety
description: A richly colored painting depicting a grand ceremonial procession. At center, a camel bears an elaborate red and gold mahmal -- an ornate pyramidal litter decorated with intricate embroidery and topped with a golden finial. The camel is adorned with rich textiles. Around it, figures in varied dress -- flowing white robes, turbans, dark coats, and fez hats -- walk in attendance. The background is suffused with warm, hazy light suggesting a Middle Eastern city. The brushwork is loose and impressionistic, capturing the pageantry and movement of the event.

_meta.title: The transfer of the sacred carpet from Mecca to Cairo
